ASBESTOS DISCOVERY 503, THE BOEING COMPANY'S PETITION TO CONDUCT INSPECTION OF SFMTA DOCUMENTS PURSUANT TO STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 2035.010 ET SEQ. THE STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER ATTACHED TO BOEING'S PETITION AS EXHIBIT E IS APPROVED AND ANY SIGNATORY TO IT MAY INSPECT AND COPY ALL DOCUMENTS PREVIOUSLY PRODUCED BY SFMTA IN RICHARD AND SUSAN HAHN'S PRIOR PERSONAL INJURY ACTION. SIGNATORIES AND SFMTA TO MEET AND CONFER ON AN INSPECTION DATE. NO OPPOSITION FILED. COUNSEL FOR THE BOEING COMPANY IS TO SUBMIT A STIPULATION AND PROPOSED PROTECTIVE ORDER FOR THE COURT'S SIGNATURE. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG, MATTER NOT REPORTED (503)

calendar.matters:
Petition Of The Boeing Company To Conduct An Inspection Of Documents Pursuant To Stipulated Protective Order Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 2035.010 Et Seq.
Off Calendar Jun-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Stephen Jenkins of MANION GAYNOR & MANNING (415-512-4381), appearing for defendant / moving party The Boeing Company

RULING:
DEFENDANT THE BOEING COMPANY'S PETITION TO CONDUCT AN INSPECTION OF DOCUMENTS PURSUANT TO STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 2035.010 ET SEQ.

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: The Boeing Company's petition to conduct inspection of SFMTA documents pursuant to stipulated protective order is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 2035.010 et seq. The stipulated protective order attached to Boeing's petition as Exhibit E is approved and any signatory to it may inspect and copy all documents previously produced by SFMTA in Richard and Susan Hahn's prior personal injury action. Signatories and SFMTA to meet and confer on an inspection date. No opposition filed. Counsel for the Boeing Company is to submit a stipulation and proposed protective order for the Court's signature. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Matter not reported (503/GLW)
